title: res.cookie with httpOnly: false — the auth cookie is readable from JavaScript and can be stolen via XSS

params.passDetail: res.cookie sets httpOnly: true — the cookie is not exposed to document.cookie, so client-side scripts cannot read the session token.
params.failDetail: res.cookie is called with httpOnly: false, which makes the cookie readable by JavaScript via document.cookie. Any XSS anywhere on the origin can exfiltrate the session/auth token and hijack the account. Set httpOnly: true for authentication cookies.
params.absentCallDetail: No res.cookie call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: res.cookie does not set httpOnly; verify auth cookies are created with httpOnly: true.

provenance.note: httpOnly: false is often set so front-end JS can read the token, trading the single most effective XSS-session-theft mitigation for convenience. It ships silently — the app still works — until an XSS turns it into full account takeover.
